title: The Laws of Human Nature
subtitle: A masterful synthesis of psychology, history, and philosophy on the hidden patterns of human behavior and how to understand and influence them.
author: Robert Greene
themes: The irrational roots of human behavior
The strategies of social influence and persuasion
The role of status, reputation, and social hierarchy
excerpt: Robert Greene, author of The 48 Laws of Power, turns his attention to the deep patterns of human behavior. Drawing on the work of thinkers from Plato to Nietzsche, and historical figures from Pericles to Martin Luther King Jr., Greene presents 18 laws that explain why we act the way we do — and how we can use this understanding to navigate social situations, manage our own emotions, and influence others effectively.
